children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate funds made available to carry out this subsection to the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico to carry out a highway program in the Commonwealth.

children.label: I
children.text: the aggregate of the amounts for the fiscal year; by

children.label: aa
children.text: the amount of funds apportioned to Puerto Rico for each such program for fiscal year 1997; bears to

children.label: bb
children.text: the total amount of funds apportioned to Puerto Rico for all such programs for fiscal year 1997.

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: ii .—Exception
children.text: Funds identified under clause (i) as having been apportioned for the national highway system, the surface transportation block grant program, and the Interstate maintenance program shall be deemed to have been apportioned 50 percent for the national highway performance program and 50 percent for the surface transportation program for purposes of imposing such penalties.

children.heading: .—Penalty
children.label: B .—Penalty
children.text: The amounts treated as being apportioned to Puerto Rico under each section referred to in subparagraph (A) shall be deemed to be required to be apportioned to Puerto Rico under that section for purposes of the imposition of any penalty under this title or title 49.

children.label: i
children.text: at least 50 percent shall be available only for purposes eligible under section 119;

children.label: ii
children.text: at least 25 percent shall be available only for purposes eligible under section 148; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any remaining funds may be obligated for activities eligible under chapter 1 and preventative maintenance on the National Highway System.

children.heading: .—Effect on apportionments
children.label: 3 .—Effect on apportionments
children.text: Except as otherwise specifically provided, Puerto Rico shall not be eligible to receive funds apportioned to States under this title.

children.label: A
children.text: American Samoa.

children.label: B
children.text: The Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.label: C
children.text: Guam.

children.label: D
children.text: The United States Virgin Islands.

children.label: i
children.text: designated by the Governor or chief executive officer of each territory; and

children.label: ii
children.text: approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Federal share
children.label: B .—Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of Federal financial assistance provided to territories under this subsection shall be in accordance with section 120(g).

children.label: i
children.text: to engage in highway planning;

children.label: ii
children.text: to conduct environmental evaluations;

children.label: iii
children.text: to administer right-of-way acquisition and relocation assistance programs; and

children.label: iv
children.text: to design, construct, operate, and maintain a system of arterial and collector highways, including necessary inter-island connectors.

children.heading: .—Form and terms of assistance
children.label: B .—Form and terms of assistance
children.text: Technical assistance provided under subparagraph (A), and the terms for the sharing of information among territories receiving the technical assistance, shall be included in the agreement required by paragraph (5).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except to the extent that provisions of this chapter are determined by the Secretary to be inconsistent with the needs of the territories and the intent of this subsection, this chapter (other than provisions of this chapter relating to the apportionment and allocation of funds) shall apply to funds made available under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Applicable provisions
children.label: B .—Applicable provisions
children.text: The agreement required by paragraph (5) for each territory shall identify the sections of this chapter that are applicable to that territory and the extent of the applicability of those sections.

children.label: i
children.text: implement the program in accordance with applicable provisions of this chapter and paragraph (4);

children.label: I
children.text: appropriate for each territory; and

children.label: II
children.text: approved by the Secretary;

children.label: iii
children.text: provide for the maintenance of facilities constructed or operated under this subsection in a condition to adequately serve the needs of present and future traffic; and

children.label: iv
children.text: implement standards for traffic operations and uniform traffic control devices that are approved by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: specify the kind of technical assistance to be provided under the program;

children.label: ii
children.text: include appropriate provisions regarding information sharing among the territories; and

children.label: iii
children.text: delineate the oversight role and responsibilities of the territories and the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Review and revision of agreement
children.label: C .—Review and revision of agreement
children.text: The agreement entered into under subparagraph (A) shall be reevaluated and, as necessary, revised, at least every 2 years.

children.label: i
children.text: the agreement shall continue in force until replaced by an agreement entered into in accordance with subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: amounts made available under this subsection under the existing agreement shall be available for obligation or expenditure so long as the agreement, or the existing agreement entered into under subparagraph (A), is in effect.

children.label: i
children.text: Eligible surface transportation block grant program projects described in section 133(b).

children.label: ii
children.text: Cost-effective, preventive maintenance consistent with section 116(e).

children.label: iii
children.text: Ferry boats, terminal facilities, and approaches, in accordance with subsections (b) and (c) of section 129.

children.label: iv
children.text: Engineering and economic surveys and investigations for the planning, and the financing, of future highway programs.

children.label: v
children.text: Studies of the economy, safety, and convenience of highway use.

children.label: vi
children.text: The regulation and equitable taxation of highway use.

children.label: vii
children.text: Such research and development as are necessary in connection with the planning, design, and maintenance of the highway system.

children.heading: .—Prohibition on use of funds for routine maintenance
children.label: B .—Prohibition on use of funds for routine maintenance
children.text: None of the funds made available under this subsection shall be obligated or expended for routine maintenance.

children.heading: .—Location of projects
children.label: 7 .—Location of projects
children.text: Territorial highway program projects (other than those described in paragraphs (1), (2), (3), and (5) of section 133(c) and section 133(b)(13)) may not be undertaken on roads functionally classified as local.
